title: Choosing the right next job
hook: The best next job is not always the most impressive one. It is the one whose tradeoffs you choose on purpose.

beats.heading: Every job offer hides a curriculum

blocks.html: A role teaches you something whether you plan it or not: speed, scale, craft, politics, product sense, resilience, or bad habits. Choose the curriculum deliberately.

blocks.title: decision · offer compare
blocks.caption: The useful signal is usually already there.

lines.t: startup: high scope, high ambiguity, lower support
platform team: strong mentorship, slower product feedback
agency: fast reps, less ownership after launch

blocks.html: Before comparing logos, decide what matters now: compensation, manager quality, learning slope, mission, location, stability, autonomy, or time outside work.

blocks.code:
| factor | weight | notes |
| manager | 5 | direct, clear examples |
| learning | 4 | systems scale work |
| comp | 3 | meets target |
blocks.caption: A scorecard will not decide for you, but it will expose your tradeoffs.

blocks.html: The recruiter sells the opportunity. Future teammates reveal the operating system: review culture, on-call reality, planning quality, and how decisions are made.

items.label: factors worth ranking before offer calls
future peers to speak with if possible

beats.heading: Choose the tradeoff you can live with

blocks.html: No job is pure upside. A good decision makes the cost visible before you sign.

items.title: What do staff engineers actually do?
